reasoning: The threshold for a positive resolution requires at least 14 protests in a 30-day window (10 x 1.333). Vietnam's strict one-party control and censorship keep reported protests very rare; the baseline average over the past year is just ~0.33 protests per month (about 4 protests all year). Reaching 14 protests in a single month would require a massive, nationwide systemic shock, similar to the 2018 Special Economic Zone protests or the 2015 Social Insurance Law strikes. Such events occur less than once every five years. Extensive web searches reveal no recent or ongoing protest waves in Vietnam as of mid-April 2025, keeping near-term probabilities extremely low (at the 0.05 floor). However, a comprehensive new Trade Union Law is scheduled to take effect on July 1, 2025. Historically, major labor or social laws can trigger localized or broader strikes around their implementation dates. Because of this, uncertainties and the corresponding probabilities increase slightly for the July and October resolution dates, though an explosion of 14+ distinct ACLED-recorded events remains highly unlikely overall.
